title: fagraea annulata
intro: Fagraea annulata, also known as the white frangipani or fragrant frangipani, is a flowering plant species native to Southeast Asia. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Fagraea annulata has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off bacterial and fungal infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
